scope_contract: In --changed-files mode this inspector emits findings ONLY for the listed files and tags each with introduced_in_diff (Mechanism ii — inspector-side tagging). Absent/false tags are treated by close.md as pre-existing/advisory/non-blocking per AC-2.6(b) default-safe rule. cleanliness scope = violations newly introduced in this diff only; pre-existing historical dirt is out of scope.

per_file_assessment.change: Prose-only edit to the 'Graphify enrichment' dispatch block (10 insertions / 4 deletions): adds a sentinel-existence guard around the graphify Agent dispatch and adds 'source venv/bin/activate &&' prefixes to two python3 invocations.
per_file_assessment.verdict: clean — command file in correct location, no temp/backup/duplicate patterns, no cleanliness violation introduced
clean — correctly located in docs/dev/, standard dev-cycle naming, not an archive candidate (active cycle)
clean — correctly located in docs/dev/, standard naming

per_file_assessment.change: Auto-generated pytest by test-writer with real regex assertions (graphify.json path + absent-sentinel prose checks against commands/dev.md). NOT an empty/placeholder test.
per_file_assessment.verdict: clean — correct location (tests/generated/<task_id>/), real assertions, tests an existing source target (commands/dev.md); not orphaned, not one-time/experimental pattern

gitignore_coverage_reasoning.assessment: No gitignore-coverage finding. tests/generated/ is intentionally NOT ignored per .gitignore (the matching .gitignore line is a comment, not an active ignore rule: 'tests/generated/ intentionally tracked — test-writer contract version-controls per-cycle pytest files'). The untracked tests/generated/ paths are therefore pending commit (tracked-by-design), not gitignore leakage. The other three harness paths (.claude/dev-registry/dev-*/, docs/codex/, .playwright-mcp/) are present as active ignore rules. No untracked path matching an ACTIVE harness rule was found. .gitignore was not in the changed-files set, so any hypothetical gap would be introduced_in_diff:false.

summary.verdict: CLEAN — no new-in-this-diff cleanliness violations across the 4 changed files; nothing forces CLOSE: NO on cleanliness grounds

codex_consult.feedback_summary: Codex (gpt-5.5, xhigh) independently verified the 4-file scope and the gitignore reasoning. It found no false positives, no missed NEW-in-diff violations, and no severity errors in the all-empty findings. It confirmed treating tests/generated/ as 'not a gitignore gap' is correct because it is an explicit tracked-by-design exception (a .gitignore comment, not an active rule), and recommended the report wording make that distinction explicit rather than implying the line is an active rule.

findings.issue: No false positives, missed NEW-in-diff cleanliness violations, or severity errors in the all-empty findings for the 4-file scope.
findings.rationale: Confirms the all-empty draft; no change required.

findings.issue: Treating tests/generated/ as not-a-gitignore-gap is correct, but the report should not imply the commented .gitignore line is an active ignore rule.
findings.proposed_fix: Revise gitignore wording to: 'tests/generated/ is intentionally not ignored per .gitignore; untracked generated pytest files are pending commit, not gitignore leakage. Other harness paths are actively ignored.'
findings.rationale: Incorporated into gitignore_coverage_reasoning.assessment — the report now explicitly states the matching .gitignore line is a comment, not an active rule, and distinguishes the three actively-ignored harness paths.

codex_consult.feedback_incorporated: Clarified the gitignore_coverage_reasoning.assessment wording per codex's PROPOSED_FIX to explicitly note that the tests/generated/ .gitignore line is a comment (intentional tracked-by-design exception), not an active ignore rule, and that the untracked generated pytest files are pending commit rather than leakage. No finding categories changed — all remain empty.
